title: penstemon leonardii var. higginsii
intro: Penstemon leonardii var. higginsii, also known as the Leonard's penstemon or white penstemon, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as iridoids and flavonoids. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations, due to its potential antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of Penstemon leonardii var. higginsii can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in topical applications, such as creams and salves, for wound healing and skin care.
